content_en:
The very day “Umar came to power, he was faced with a critical, not to mention urgent, situation that called for action. Along Abu Bakr's two-year caliphate, he continued what the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, had started. Muslim armies were sent both to Persia and Constantinople to invite people to embrace Islam, It is indeed imperative to point that that was Muslims' single goal in launching Jihad. Notwithstanding Muslims’ peaceful intentions, the Persian and the Byzantines met them with fierce hostility and their call to the Right Path was faced by decided contempt. While Abu Bakr was on his deathbed, the political arena was going through a critical juncture. The Emperor of Syria prepared a massive army to face Muslim troops and teach them a lesson. Abu Bakr therefore had to send the necessary succor to Syria. He commanded Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed to lead some troops and rush to their brethern's aid, leaving Al-Muthannah Ibn Harithah AshShibani in charge of the remaining troops in Iraq. Taking advantage of the situation, the Khosrau of Persia mobilised a huge army to deal Muslims a heavy blow and expel them from Iraq, which, for many years, had been under Persian control.

Feeling his troops were in impending jeopardy, Al-Muthannah had another man fill in for him and hurried to Madinah to seek the council of the Commander of the Faithful. In his last hours, Abu Bakr asked “Umar to call on people to join forces and head for Iraq to engage in Jihad.

*Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab Takes Over:

The first thing that ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattab did upon his becoming the Caliph was deposing Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed from the general command of the army. ‘Umar appointed Abu “Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraaah . Further, ~Umar ordered Abu ~Ubaydah to send back to Iraq all soldiers who had moved to Syria earlier, in order to join the armies headed by Sa*d Ibn Abi Waqqaas in his war against the Persians. Khalid himself would remain under Abu *Ubaydah's command in Syria. With the Muslim troops preparing to launch yet another battle against the Byzantines, Abu ~Ubaydah, confident of Allah's victory under the command of Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, realized the risk he would be taking by disclosing the news of the change of the command. Driven by much laudable prudence and insights, Muslims' new leader kept the news of Khalid's deposition a secret to avoid even the slightest chance of disruption among the ranks of their troops. It is reported that twenty days after Damascus was conquered, Khalid learned the news of his deposition and asked Abu *Ubaydah: "May Allah have mercy on you! Why did you not inform us of the content of the message of the Commander of the Faithful when you received i?" Abu ~Ubaydah replied: "I hated to ruin your war strategem. I am not after worldly power, nor is it worldly gains that I seek. All that we have accomplished and gained is bound to come to an end or perish. We are brothers and it never harms a man to have his brother replace him in shouldering the responsibilities of religion as well as worldly affairs." Quite an exceptional man Abu “Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraaah was!

Appointing him as the leader of Muslims troops only unravels ~Umar's wisdom and insight.

The Battle of Al-Yarmook:

The next day after Abu ~ Ubaydah received the message of the Commander of the Faithful, Muslim troops, under the command of Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, marched to Al-Yarmook. Fierce fighting broke out. The Byzantines fought adamantly, filled with bitter animosity towards the Arab desert-dwellers who turned their lives into a living hell. The Muslims, on the other hand, fought dauntlessly spurred by a genuine desire to achieve victory and uphold Islam. However, at a certain point, it was difficult to predict which of the two sides was to have the upper hand, In the eyes of Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, the Muslims had but a single choice, namely to achieve victory. Otherwise, they would be running the risk of being defeated and consequently getting detached from any succor. This being the case, they displayed exemplary boldness and intrepidity that could only have been rewarded by Allah's definite victory. The Muslims' great victory at Al-Yarmook was only the beginning of a long line of triumphs in Syria.

It was then that Abu * Ubaydah realized that it was about time he announced the news of Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed's deposition. Yet, he was torn apart, wondering how to best break the news. At that point, the news of Abu Bakr's death and ~Umar's assuming power had reached soldiers in Syria. Khalid immediately figured out that the new Caliph would never let him continue to lead the Muslim troops in war. He therefore gathered a number of his close friends, including Abu *Ubaydah, and shared with them his worries. Abu ~ Ubaydah thought the time was appropriate to disclose the secret he had been keeping. Thereupon, Khalid displayed total submission to, and acceptance of ~ Umar's command, without getting furious or

even feeling humiliated. He did not hesitate to command his small brigade under the general command of Abu * Ubaydah.

Abu *Ubaydah departed from Al-Yarmook meaning to head for Damascus and besiege the city. However, he learned that the garrison of Damascus were succored by troops dispatched from Hims and that large hosts of the Byzantines were gathered at Fahl in Palestine. Not knowing where to head for first, Abu “Ubaydah wrote to ‘Umar seeking his advice. The Commander of the Faithful wrote back saying: "Start with Damascus, Syria's stronghold and the headquarters of their kingdom and fight its people. In the meantime, keep people of Fahl busy with as many squadrons as available. Should you manage to conquer Fahl before Damascus, we would be pleased. Should you conquer Damascus first, proceed, along with your leaders, to conquer Fahl. If Allah grants you victory and when you have conquered Fahl, head afterwards for Hims along with Khalid and leave Jordan and Palestine in charge of * Amr and Sharhabeel."

Upon receiving “Umar's message, Abu ~Ubaydah dispatched ten of his leaders along with their troops to Fahl, whereas he headed, together with Khaild, to Damascus leading a massive army of fearless soldiers, Damascus was a wellfortified city thanks to the large number of soldiers Heraclius had marched to from Hims. The Byzantine emperor was under the illusion that the Muslims would give in and abandon the idea of conquering Damascus. However, the Muslim troops rigorously besieged the city for a long period of time that lasted for four, six, or fourteen months, as was reported in different narrations. Now the commander of all Muslim troops in Syria, Abu ~*Ubaydah stationed his troops before the great gates of the city.

Damascus was then ruled by Nestas. When Muslims

arrived there, Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed positioned his troops by the eastern gate and Kisan gate, Abu * Ubaydah by the great Gabiyah gate and Yazeed Ibn Abu Sufyaan by the small gate, whereas “Amr Ibn Al-* Aas and Sharhabeel Ibn Hasanah were in charge of the rest of the gates. Abu ~ Ubaydah also had DhalKalla’ at the head of an army situated between Damascus and Hims to intervene whatever succor Heraclius might send them. The Muslims besieged Damascus for seventy days. According to other books, the blockade lasted for four, six or even fourteen months. The people of Damascus adamantly refused to give in. They sent to their emperor in Hims asking for succor; yet none arrived on account of Dhal-Kalla*'s army, positioned a short distance from Damascus. When the people of Damascus realized that there was no way they would ever receive succuor, they grew weaker. Conversely, Muslims grew stronger and their blockade more rigorous.

With the advent of winter and cold weather, the situation got worse. One night a boy was born to the patriarch of Damascus. He therefore held a huge banquet to celebrate the happy occasion. The guards of the gates indulged in food and drink, celebrated and revelled all night long and fell asleep afterwards, leaving the gates unguarded. It was none other than Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed who took advantage of the situation, as he was vigilant all the time and never let any of his soldiers fall asleep. He had eyes spread all around the place to report to him day and night. When he learned of the joyous occasion they were celebrating that night and realized that guards had abandoned their positions, he set a plan. He, along with some of the intrepid Muslims, swam across the trench outside the wall. They then climbed up the wall with the aid of ropes and descended on the other side of it. The Muslim soldiers met very little resistance, as there were but a few guards, unable to get up on their feet because of the hefty meal they had. The Muslim soldiers easily killed them all and proceeded to the city gates,

which they broke open.

When the people of Damascus heard the Muslims cry, " Allah is the Greatest", they were filled with terror. They then asked Muslim leaders at each gate for a reconciliation, though they had obstinately refused to give in before. The Muslims then answered their request, not knowing what Khalid Ibn AlWaleed and his assistants had accomplished on the other side of the city. They then made their way into Damascus through all of its gates. The Muslims encountered Khalid killing residents of the city and informed him that they had signed a settlement with the city residents to spare their lives. Having conquered Damascus, the capital of the Byzantines, the way seemed paved for the triumphant Muslims to subject the entire of Syria to their command.

The Muslims wrote to the Commander of the Faithful informing him of their victory. Exulted by the good news, *Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab wrote back commanding Abu * Ubaydah to pursue their conquests with a view to ridding Syria of the Byzantines and annexing it to the Muslim state. Consequently, Abu *Ubaydah and Khalid managed to conquer Fahl. Bissan readily surrendered while Tabariah was granted the settlement it requested.

The Commander of the Faithful then commanded Abu ~Ubaydah to head for Hims and invade it. Abu * Ubaydah and Khalid imposed a rigorous blockade on the city, whose residents stubbornly refused to give in. They were counting on what they believed to be the soon-to-arrive succor from Heraclius. Otherwise, the Muslims, they conjectured, would not be able to endure the cold weather any longer. That is why they deliberately chose to engage in quick skirmishes on the particularly freezing cold days. However, the Byzantines were to face a tremendous disappointment. No succor arrived, nor

did the Muslim army even flinch from the cold. Consequently, the siege continued to the point that some of the residents of Hims began to contemplate the possibility of signing a settlement with Muslims. This came as a shock to residents of the opposing side in Hims who were of the view that this would be a definite act of disgrace and that they should never give in to the vicious invaders. However, contrary to all expectations, the blockade came to an end thanks to the Hand of Providence. A severe earthquake hit the area pulling down the wall surrounding the city as well as houses inside. With chaos spread all around, and people of Hims panic-stricken, they desperately pleaded for a settlement. Seeing that his troops were weary and cold-stricken, Abu ~Ubaydah readily signed the settlement and entered the city.
content_id:
Pada hari 'Umar (ra) berkuasa, beliau dihadapkan pada situasi yang kritis, belum lagi mendesak, yang memerlukan tindakan segera. Sepanjang kekhalifahan Abu Bakr (ra) selama dua tahun, beliau meneruskan apa yang telah dimulai oleh Nabi ﷺ. Pasukan Muslim dikirim baik ke Persia maupun Konstantinopel untuk mengajak orang-orang memeluk Islam. Sungguh penting untuk ditegaskan bahwa itulah satu-satunya tujuan kaum Muslimin dalam melancarkan Jihad. Meskipun demikian, niat damai kaum Muslimin disambut dengan permusuhan yang sengit oleh orang-orang Persia dan Byzantium, dan seruan mereka menuju Jalan yang Benar disambut dengan penghinaan yang nyata. Sementara Abu Bakr (ra) berada di ranjang kematiannya, arena politik sedang melalui persimpangan yang kritis. Kaisar Syam mempersiapkan pasukan besar untuk menghadapi pasukan Muslim dan mengajari mereka pelajaran. Oleh karena itu, Abu Bakr (ra) harus mengirimkan bala bantuan yang diperlukan ke Syam. Beliau memerintahkan Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) untuk memimpin sejumlah pasukan dan segera membantu saudara-saudara mereka, meninggalkan Al-Mutsanna Ibn Haritsah Asy-Syaibani sebagai penanggung jawab pasukan yang tersisa di Irak. Memanfaatkan situasi tersebut, Kisra Persia memobilisasi pasukan besar untuk memberikan pukulan telak kepada kaum Muslimin dan mengusir mereka dari Irak, yang selama bertahun-tahun berada di bawah kendali Persia.

Merasa pasukannya dalam bahaya yang mengancam, Al-Mutsanna menyuruh orang lain menggantikannya dan bergegas ke Madinah untuk meminta nasihat dari Amirul Mukminin. Dalam jam-jam terakhirnya, Abu Bakr (ra) meminta 'Umar (ra) untuk mengajak orang-orang bergabung dan menuju Irak untuk berjihad.

'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) Mengambil Alih Kepemimpinan:

Hal pertama yang dilakukan 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) setelah menjadi khalifah adalah memecat Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) dari komando umum pasukan. 'Umar (ra) menunjuk Abu 'Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah (ra). Lebih lanjut, 'Umar (ra) memerintahkan Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) untuk mengirim kembali ke Irak semua tentara yang telah pindah ke Syam sebelumnya, untuk bergabung dengan pasukan yang dipimpin oleh Sa'd Ibn Abi Waqqash (ra) dalam perangnya melawan orang-orang Persia. Khalid (ra) sendiri akan tetap berada di bawah komando Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) di Syam. Dengan pasukan Muslim mempersiapkan diri untuk melancarkan pertempuran baru melawan Byzantium, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra), yang yakin akan kemenangan Allah di bawah komando Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra), menyadari risiko yang akan diambilnya jika mengungkapkan berita tentang pergantian komando. Didorong oleh kehati-hatian dan kecerdasan yang sangat terpuji, pemimpin baru kaum Muslimin menyimpan berita pemecatan Khalid (ra) sebagai rahasia untuk menghindari kemungkinan terjadinya gangguan di antara barisan pasukan mereka. Dilaporkan bahwa dua puluh hari setelah Damaskus ditaklukkan, Khalid (ra) mengetahui berita pemecatannya dan bertanya kepada Abu 'Ubaidah (ra): "Semoga Allah merahmatimu! Mengapa kamu tidak memberitahu kami tentang isi pesan Amirul Mukminin ketika kamu menerimanya?" Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menjawab: "Aku tidak ingin merusak strategi perangmu. Aku tidak mencari kekuasaan duniawi, bukan pula keuntungan duniawi yang aku cari. Semua yang telah kita capai dan raih pasti akan berakhir atau binasa. Kita adalah saudara dan tidak ada ruginya bagi seseorang jika saudaranya menggantikannya dalam memikul tanggung jawab agama maupun urusan duniawi." Sungguh orang yang luar biasa, Abu 'Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah (ra)!

Penunjukannya sebagai pemimpin pasukan Muslim hanya mengungkapkan kebijaksanaan dan wawasan 'Umar (ra).

Pertempuran Al-Yarmuk:

Keesokan harinya setelah Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menerima pesan Amirul Mukminin, pasukan Muslim di bawah komando Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) bergerak menuju Al-Yarmuk. Pertempuran sengit pun meletus. Pasukan Byzantium bertempur dengan gigih, dipenuhi kebencian yang mendalam terhadap para penghuni gurun Arab yang membuat hidup mereka seperti neraka. Kaum Muslimin, di pihak lain, bertempur tanpa kenal takut, didorong oleh keinginan tulus untuk meraih kemenangan dan menegakkan Islam. Namun, pada suatu titik, sulit untuk memprediksi pihak mana yang akan unggul. Dalam pandangan Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra), kaum Muslimin hanya punya satu pilihan, yakni meraih kemenangan. Jika tidak, mereka berisiko dikalahkan dan akibatnya terputus dari segala bantuan. Menghadapi situasi demikian, mereka menampilkan keberanian dan keteguhan yang luar biasa yang hanya bisa dibalas dengan kemenangan pasti dari Allah. Kemenangan besar kaum Muslimin di Al-Yarmuk hanyalah awal dari serangkaian panjang kemenangan di Syam.

Saat itulah Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menyadari bahwa sudah waktunya untuk mengumumkan berita pemecatan Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra). Namun, beliau diliputi kebingungan, bertanya-tanya bagaimana cara terbaik untuk menyampaikan berita tersebut. Pada saat itu, berita wafatnya Abu Bakr (ra) dan pengambilalihan kekuasaan oleh 'Umar (ra) telah sampai kepada para tentara di Syam. Khalid (ra) segera menyadari bahwa khalifah baru tidak akan pernah membiarkannya terus memimpin pasukan Muslim dalam perang. Beliau kemudian mengumpulkan beberapa teman dekatnya, termasuk Abu 'Ubaidah (ra), dan berbagi kekhawatirannya. Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) berpikir bahwa waktu yang tepat telah tiba untuk mengungkapkan rahasia yang selama ini beliau simpan. Khalid (ra) pun menunjukkan ketundukan dan penerimaan total terhadap perintah 'Umar (ra), tanpa marah atau merasa dipermalukan. Beliau tidak ragu-ragu untuk memimpin brigade kecilnya di bawah komando umum Abu 'Ubaidah (ra).

Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) berangkat dari Al-Yarmuk dengan tujuan menuju Damaskus untuk mengepungnya. Namun, beliau mengetahui bahwa garnisun Damaskus mendapat bala bantuan dari pasukan yang dikirim dari Hims dan bahwa sejumlah besar pasukan Byzantium berkumpul di Fahl di Palestina. Tidak tahu ke mana harus pergi terlebih dahulu, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menulis kepada 'Umar (ra) untuk meminta saran. Amirul Mukminin membalas: "Mulailah dengan Damaskus, benteng Syam dan pusat kerajaan mereka, dan perangilah penduduknya. Sementara itu, sibukkan penduduk Fahl dengan sebanyak mungkin pasukan yang tersedia. Jika kamu berhasil menaklukkan Fahl sebelum Damaskus, kami akan senang. Jika kamu menaklukkan Damaskus lebih dulu, lanjutkan bersama para pemimpinmu untuk menaklukkan Fahl. Jika Allah menganugerahkan kemenangan kepadamu dan ketika kamu telah menaklukkan Fahl, teruslah menuju Hims bersama Khalid dan serahkan Yordania dan Palestina kepada 'Amr dan Syarhabeel."

Setelah menerima pesan 'Umar (ra), Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) mengirim sepuluh pemimpin beserta pasukan mereka ke Fahl, sementara beliau sendiri bersama Khalid (ra) menuju Damaskus memimpin pasukan besar yang terdiri dari para prajurit pemberani. Damaskus adalah kota yang sangat berbenteng berkat banyaknya tentara yang dikirim Heraklius dari Hims. Kaisar Byzantium berilusi bahwa kaum Muslimin akan menyerah dan meninggalkan gagasan menaklukkan Damaskus. Namun, pasukan Muslim mengepung kota itu dengan ketat selama waktu yang panjang, berlangsung empat, enam, atau empat belas bulan, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam berbagai riwayat. Sebagai komandan seluruh pasukan Muslim di Syam, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menempatkan pasukannya di depan gerbang-gerbang besar kota itu.

Damaskus saat itu diperintah oleh Nestas. Ketika kaum Muslimin tiba di sana, Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) memposisikan pasukannya di pintu timur dan pintu Kisan, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) di pintu Jabiyah yang besar dan Yazid Ibn Abu Sufyan di pintu kecil, sementara 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra) dan Syarhabeel Ibn Hasanah bertanggung jawab atas gerbang-gerbang lainnya. Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) juga menempatkan Dzul-Kalla' di kepala pasukan yang berada di antara Damaskus dan Hims untuk mencegat bala bantuan yang mungkin dikirim Heraklius. Kaum Muslimin mengepung Damaskus selama tujuh puluh hari. Menurut buku-buku lain, blokade berlangsung empat, enam, atau bahkan empat belas bulan. Penduduk Damaskus dengan keras kepala menolak untuk menyerah. Mereka mengirim pesan kepada kaisar mereka di Hims meminta bala bantuan, namun tidak ada yang datang karena pasukan Dzul-Kalla' yang berposisi tidak jauh dari Damaskus. Ketika penduduk Damaskus menyadari bahwa tidak ada cara mereka akan pernah menerima bala bantuan, mereka semakin lemah. Sebaliknya, kaum Muslimin semakin kuat dan blokade mereka semakin ketat.

Dengan datangnya musim dingin dan cuaca yang dingin, situasi semakin memburuk. Suatu malam, seorang anak laki-laki lahir dari keluarga patriark Damaskus. Maka ia mengadakan pesta besar untuk merayakan acara yang membahagiakan tersebut. Para penjaga gerbang larut dalam makanan dan minuman, merayakan dan berpesta sepanjang malam lalu tertidur sesudahnya, meninggalkan gerbang tanpa penjagaan. Tidak lain dan tidak bukan Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) yang memanfaatkan situasi tersebut, karena beliau waspada setiap saat dan tidak pernah membiarkan seorang pun tentaranya tertidur. Beliau menyebar mata-mata di sekeliling tempat untuk melapor kepadanya siang dan malam. Ketika beliau mengetahui tentang perayaan yang mereka lakukan malam itu dan menyadari bahwa para penjaga telah meninggalkan posisi mereka, beliau menyusun sebuah rencana. Beliau, bersama beberapa Muslim yang pemberani, berenang menyeberangi parit di luar tembok. Mereka kemudian memanjat tembok dengan bantuan tali dan turun ke sisi lainnya. Para tentara Muslim hampir tidak menemui perlawanan, karena hanya ada sedikit penjaga yang tidak bisa berdiri karena makanan yang terlalu banyak yang mereka konsumsi. Para tentara Muslim dengan mudah membunuh mereka semua dan melanjutkan ke gerbang kota yang mereka dobrak terbuka.

Ketika penduduk Damaskus mendengar teriakan kaum Muslimin, "Allahu Akbar", mereka dipenuhi ketakutan. Mereka kemudian meminta perdamaian dari para pemimpin Muslim di setiap gerbang, meskipun sebelumnya mereka dengan keras kepala menolak untuk menyerah. Kaum Muslimin pun menjawab permintaan mereka, tanpa mengetahui apa yang telah Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) dan para pembantunya capai di sisi lain kota itu. Mereka kemudian masuk ke Damaskus melalui semua gerbangnya. Kaum Muslimin berjumpa dengan Khalid (ra) yang sedang membunuh penduduk kota dan memberitahunya bahwa mereka telah menandatangani perjanjian damai dengan penduduk kota untuk menyelamatkan nyawa mereka. Setelah menaklukkan Damaskus, ibu kota Byzantium, jalan tampaknya terbuka bagi kaum Muslimin yang menang untuk menundukkan seluruh Syam di bawah kendali mereka.

Kaum Muslimin menulis kepada Amirul Mukminin memberitahukan kemenangan mereka. Bergembira dengan berita baik tersebut, 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) membalas dengan memerintahkan Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) untuk melanjutkan penaklukan dengan tujuan membebaskan Syam dari Byzantium dan menggabungkannya ke dalam negara Islam. Akibatnya, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) dan Khalid (ra) berhasil menaklukkan Fahl. Bissan dengan suka rela menyerahkan diri sementara Tabariyah mendapatkan perjanjian perdamaian yang dimintanya.

Amirul Mukminin kemudian memerintahkan Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) untuk menuju Hims dan menyerangnya. Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) dan Khalid (ra) memberlakukan blokade yang ketat terhadap kota tersebut, yang penduduknya dengan keras kepala menolak untuk menyerah. Mereka mengandalkan apa yang mereka yakini akan segera datang berupa bala bantuan dari Heraklius. Selain itu, mereka berpikir bahwa kaum Muslimin tidak akan bisa tahan lagi menghadapi cuaca dingin. Itulah mengapa mereka sengaja memilih untuk terlibat dalam pertempuran singkat di hari-hari yang sangat dingin. Namun, orang-orang Byzantium akan menghadapi kekecewaan yang luar biasa. Tidak ada bala bantuan yang datang, dan pasukan Muslim pun tidak bergeming sedikit pun dari kedinginan. Akibatnya, pengepungan terus berlanjut hingga beberapa penduduk Hims mulai memikirkan kemungkinan untuk menandatangani perjanjian damai dengan kaum Muslimin. Hal ini mengejutkan penduduk dari pihak yang berlawanan di Hims yang berpendapat bahwa ini pasti merupakan tindakan yang memalukan dan mereka tidak boleh menyerah kepada para penyerbu yang jahat. Namun, bertolak belakang dengan semua perkiraan, blokade itu berakhir berkat Kehendak Allah. Sebuah gempa bumi dahsyat melanda daerah tersebut, meruntuhkan tembok yang mengelilingi kota serta rumah-rumah di dalamnya. Dengan kekacauan yang menyebar ke mana-mana dan penduduk Hims yang panik, mereka putus asa memohon perdamaian. Melihat pasukannya kelelahan dan kedinginan, Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) segera menandatangani perjanjian dan memasuki kota.
content_ar: The very day “عمر came to power, he was faced with a البالغ الأهمية, not to mention urgent, situation that called for action. Along أبو بكر's two-year الخلافة, he continued what النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, had started. مسلم armies were sent both to Persia and Constantinople to invite people to embrace الإسلام, It is indeed imperative to point that that was المسلمون' single goal in launching Jihad. Notwithstanding المسلمون’ peaceful intentions, the Persian and the Byzantines met them with fierce hostility and their call to the الحق الطريق was faced by decided contempt. بينما أبو بكر was on his deathbed, the political arena was going through a البالغ الأهمية juncture. The Emperor of Syria prepared a massive army to face مسلم troops and teach them a lesson. أبو بكر لذلك had to send the necessary succor to Syria. He commanded Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed to lead some troops and rush to their brethern's aid, leaving Al-Muthannah Ibn Harithah AshShibani in charge of the remaining troops in Iraq. Taking advantage of the situation, the Khosrau of Persia mobilised a huge army to deal المسلمون a heavy blow and expel them from Iraq, which, for many years, had been under Persian control. Feeling his troops were in impending jeopardy, Al-Muthannah had another man fill in for him and hurried to المدينة المنورة to seek the council of the Commander of the Faithful. In his last hours, أبو بكر asked “عمر to call on people to join forces and head for Iraq to engage in Jihad. *عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab Takes Over: The أولاً thing that ~عمر Ibn Al-Khattab did upon his becoming the Caliph was deposing Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed from the general command of the army. ‘عمر appointed Abu “Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraaah . Further, ~عمر ordered Abu ~Ubaydah to send back to Iraq all soldiers who had moved to Syria earlier, من أجل join the armies headed by Sa*d Ibn Abi Waqqaas in his war against the Persians. Khalid himself would remain under Abu *Ubaydah's command in Syria. With the مسلم troops preparing to launch yet another battle against the Byzantines, Abu ~Ubaydah, confident of الله's victory under the command of Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, realized the risk he would be taking by disclosing the news of the change of the command. Driven by much laudable prudence and insights, المسلمون' new leader kept the news of Khalid's deposition a secret to avoid even the slightest chance of disruption among the ranks of their troops. وردت الرواية أنه twenty days بعد Damascus was conquered, Khalid learned the news of his deposition and asked Abu *Ubaydah: "May الله have الرحمة on you! Why did you not inform us of the content of the message of the Commander of the Faithful عندما you received i?" Abu ~Ubaydah replied: "I hated to ruin your war strategem. I am not بعد worldly power, nor is it worldly gains that I seek. All that we have accomplished and gained is bound to come to an end or perish. We are brothers and it never harms a man to have his الأخ replace him i
